get.description:
Redacted cross-org audit feed for the platform admin. Returns ALL audit row
types — resource provisioning, secrets, and governance events — with
sensitive fields stripped by construction (see [`RedactedAuditEntry`]).

Unlike `/api/v1/audit-logs`, this endpoint does NOT coerce the org filter to
the caller's own org and does NOT exclude the platform/default org:
governance rows and `org_id IS NULL` rows are explicitly visible here.

Requires `admin:all`.

Audit-event volume per org for the requested window, with a delta against
the trailing 7-day daily average. Requires `admin:all`.

Per-org filter-type counts. The taxonomy is derived from
`FilterType::all()` — never hand-listed. Requires `admin:all`.

Fleet-wide count of active MCP connections (SSE) and sessions (HTTP). This
is an in-memory rollup — no database query. Requires `admin:all`.
Most recent config-mutating audit event per org. Requires `admin:all`.

Recent resource-provisioning events (cluster/listener create, dataplane
register) inside the window, newest first. Requires `admin:all`.

Returns aggregated resource counts per team and org for the platform admin dashboard.
Requires `admin:all` scope.

Returns all scopes including those not visible in UI (like admin:all).
This endpoint requires admin access and is useful for:
- Auditing available permissions
- Debugging scope issues
- Managing scope visibility

get.summary: List all teams with pagination (admin only).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return (default: 50)

get.summary: Get a team by ID (admin only).

put.description: Updates team details. Note that the team name is immutable and cannot be changed.

delete.description:
Deletes a team. This operation will fail if there are resources (listeners, routes,
clusters, etc.) referencing this team due to foreign key constraints.

get.description:
Fleet-wide xDS health rollup: per-dataplane connection state, last config
verify, and NACK count. Backs the F1 "Control plane health" KPI tile and
the F4 sidebar pill. Requires `admin:all`.

get.summary: List audit logs with optional filtering and pagination
get.description: **Admin only**: This endpoint requires admin:all scope

post.summary: Bootstrap initialization — creates platform governance org/team and the default org/team.
post.description:
Always creates the "platform" org with "platform-admin" team (idempotent).
Then creates the requested org and team for regular users.

This endpoint is idempotent for platform org/team creation. Calling it again after
a non-platform organization already exists returns a conflict error.

The endpoint is gated by the one-time bootstrap token (`FLOWPLANE_BOOTSTRAP_TOKEN`)
— see [`verify_bootstrap_token`]. It is not on the JWT auth path because it runs
before any org admin exists.

get.description:
Checks whether the system needs initialization by verifying that
the Zitadel project ID is configured and at least one organization
exists (beyond the default platform org).

Returns the current mTLS configuration status for the control plane.
This endpoint helps operators and developers understand whether mTLS
is enabled and properly configured.

# Response Fields

- `enabled`: True if both PKI and xDS TLS are configured
- `xds_server_tls`: True if xDS server has TLS certificate configured
- `client_auth_required`: True if client certificates are required
- `trust_domain`: The SPIFFE trust domain being used
- `pki_mount_configured`: True if Vault PKI is configured for cert generation

delete.summary: Delete import and cascade to routes/clusters/listeners

get.description:
List the whole org's audit log entries (org-admin lens). The org filter is
taken from the caller's auth context — callers cannot widen the scope.
Rolling-window audit event counts across the whole org (org-admin lens).

get.summary: List all route flows showing end-to-end request routing
get.description:
This endpoint provides a comprehensive view of how requests flow through the system:
listener → route → cluster → endpoint

# Authorization
- Requires `reports:read` scope
- Team-scoped tokens only see their team's routes
- `admin:all` scope grants access to all routes across teams

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return (1-1000, default: 50)

parameters.name: team
parameters.description: Filter by team name (optional, admin tokens can filter by any team)

get.summary: List routes with pagination, search, and filtering for UI display.
get.description:
Returns a flattened view of routes with computed fields extracted from
configuration JSON. Supports pagination, search, and MCP filter query params.

This handler uses an optimized JOIN query to fetch all related data in a single
database call, avoiding the N+1 query problem.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Number of items per page (default: 20, max: 100)

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search query (searches name, path, domain, cluster)

parameters.name: mcpFilter
parameters.description: Filter by MCP status ("enabled", "disabled", or null for all)

200.description: Paginated list of routes with stats

get.summary: Get statistics for the route list view.
get.description: Returns aggregate statistics for the current team's routes.

Returns all UI-visible scopes that can be assigned to tokens.
This endpoint is public (no authentication required) since users need
to see available scopes when creating or viewing tokens.

Scopes that are marked as not visible in UI (like admin:all) are excluded.

get.summary: List teams accessible to the current user
get.description:
Returns:
- All teams (from teams table) if user is admin
- Only user's teams (from memberships) if user is not admin

get.summary: List aggregated schemas with optional filters

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Text search in API path (substring match)

parameters.name: minConfidence
parameters.description: Filter by minimum confidence score (0.0 to 1.0)

parameters.name: withVersion
parameters.description: Version number to compare with current schema

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return (default: 50)

get.summary: List custom WASM filters for a team

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return (default: 50)

post.summary: Create a new custom WASM filter

409.description: Filter with this name already exists

get.summary: Get a custom WASM filter by ID

put.summary: Update a custom WASM filter's metadata

get.summary: Download the WASM binary for a custom filter

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return (default: 50)

400.description: Validation error - filter not installed on relevant listeners

409.description: Filter already configured for this scope

parameters.name: scope_type
parameters.description: Scope type: route-config, virtual-host, or route

404.description: Filter, scope, or configuration not found

400.description: Validation error - filter type incompatible with listener

409.description: Filter already installed on this listener

404.description: Filter, listener, or installation not found

200.description: Filter status with installations and configurations

400.description: Invalid state transition - session already completed, cancelled, or failed

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return (default: 50)

post.summary: Import OpenAPI spec and materialize routes directly to routes table

parameters.name: listener_mode
parameters.description: Listener mode: "existing" to use an existing listener, "new" to create a new one

parameters.name: existing_listener_name
parameters.description: Name of an existing listener to use (required when listener_mode="existing")

parameters.name: new_listener_name
parameters.description: Name for the new listener (required when listener_mode="new")

parameters.name: new_listener_address
parameters.description: Bind address for the new listener (default: 0.0.0.0)

parameters.name: dataplane_id
parameters.description: Dataplane ID for the new listener (required when listener_mode="new")

requestBody.description: OpenAPI 3.0 document in JSON or YAML format

get.summary: List all imports for a team

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return (default: 50)

get.summary: List all virtual hosts for a route config
List filters attached to a virtual host

200.description: List of filters attached to virtual host

404.description: Route config or virtual host not found

post.summary: Attach a filter to a virtual host

404.description: Route config, virtual host, or filter not found

delete.summary: Detach a filter from a virtual host

200.description: List of routes in virtual host

404.description: Route config or virtual host not found

200.description: List of filters attached to route

404.description: Route config, virtual host, or route not found

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of secrets to return (default: 50)

503.description: Secret repository unavailable or backend unavailable

get.description:
Returns 200 OK when the API server is operational.
This endpoint is unauthenticated and suitable for:
- Kubernetes liveness/readiness probes
- Docker healthchecks
- Load balancer health checks
- Monitoring systems

AttachFilterRequest.description: Request to attach a filter to a virtual host or route rule

filterId.description: ID of the filter to attach

order.description: Optional order for the filter (defaults to next available order)

AttachmentPoint.description:
Represents the resource types that a filter can attach to.
Different filter types have different valid attachment points based on their scope.

AuditLogEntry.description: Audit log entry returned from database queries.

severity.description:
Severity tier, derived from `action` at read time — there is no
`severity` column (derive-from-verb, fp-0ees.3).

AuditVolumeResponse.description: Audit-volume rollup across all tenant orgs.

AuthSecretConfig.description:
Secret reference for API key/token authentication with the ext_authz service.
The secret is delivered via SDS (Secret Discovery Service).

name.description: Name of the SDS secret containing the auth credential (API key, bearer token, etc.)

allowed_headers.description: Headers to include from the original request

headers_to_add.description: Headers to add to the authorization request

allowed_client_headers.description: Headers from authz response to add to the client response on success

allowed_client_headers_on_success.description: Headers from authz response to add to the client response on denial

allowed_upstream_headers.description: Headers to add to the upstream request on success

Base64Bytes.description:
Wrapper for binary protobuf payloads serialized as base64 in JSON.

Used to represent Envoy protobuf `Any` message values in JSON APIs.
Automatically handles base64 encoding/decoding during serialization.

displayName.description: Human-readable display name for the organization.

teamName.description: Initial team name to create within the org.

defaultDataplane.description:
Actual name of the team's default dataplane, which a successful bootstrap
guarantees exists (via `reconcile_default_dataplane_tx`). Usually `default`,
but reported as the real name rather than assumed.

maxPendingRequests.description: Maximum number of pending requests allowed.

ClusterCreationConfig.description:
Configuration for cluster creation or reuse when creating a filter

Filters like OAuth2, JWT Auth, and ExtAuthz require clusters for their
backend services. This config allows creating a new cluster inline or
reusing an existing one.

clusterName.description: Name of the cluster to create or reuse

mode.description: Mode: "create" to create a new cluster, "reuse" to use existing

serviceName.description: Service name for the cluster (required when mode is "create")

team.description: Team for the cluster (defaults to filter's team if not specified)

ClusterMode.description: Mode for cluster handling when creating filters

protocolType.description:
Protocol type for upstream connections.
Set to "HTTP2" or "GRPC" for gRPC/HTTP2 upstreams (e.g., OTEL collectors).
Defaults to HTTP/1.1 if not specified.

content_type.description: Content types that should be compressed.

disable_on_etag_header.description: Whether to disable compression on responses with ETag header.

min_content_length.description: Minimum response size in bytes to trigger compression.

remove_accept_encoding_header.description: Whether to remove Accept-Encoding header after compression decision.

withVersion.description: Version number to compare with current schema

ConfigureFilterRequest.description: Request body for configuring filter scope

scopeId.description: ID or name of the scope resource

settings.description: Optional per-route/vhost settings (e.g., disabled: true, or override config)

CorsConfig.description: Filter-level configuration wrapper (typed config on HttpFilter entries).

CorsOriginMatcher.description: Representation of a single CORS origin match rule using Envoy's `StringMatcher`.

allow_headers.description: Request headers allowed in cross-origin requests.

allow_methods.description: HTTP methods allowed for cross-origin requests.

allow_origin.description: String matchers describing allowed origins. Must not be empty.

allow_private_network_access.description: Allow requests targeting a more private network.

expose_headers.description: Response headers exposed to cross-origin clients.

oneOf.description: Percentage of requests where filter is enforced.

forward_not_matching_preflights.description: Forward preflight requests that do not match configured origins.

max_age.description: Max age in seconds for preflight cache. Optional.

oneOf.description: Percentage of requests where policy is evaluated but not enforced.

circuitBreakers.description: Circuit breaker thresholds applied to the cluster.

dnsLookupFamily.description: DNS lookup family for hostname endpoints (`AUTO`, `V4_ONLY`, `V6_ONLY`, `V4_PREFERRED`, `ALL`).

endpoints.description: Upstream endpoints (host & port) for this cluster. At least one is required.

name.description: Unique name for the cluster.

protocolType.description:
Protocol type for upstream connections.
Use "HTTP2" or "GRPC" for gRPC/HTTP2 upstreams (e.g., OTEL collectors, gRPC services).
Defaults to HTTP/1.1 if not specified.

serviceName.description: Optional service identifier exposed to clients (defaults to `name`).

tlsServerName.description: Optional SNI server name to present during TLS handshake.

useTls.description: Enable TLS for upstream connections (default: false).

CreateCustomWasmFilterRequest.description: Request to create a custom WASM filter (JSON API)

config_schema.description: JSON Schema for validating filter configuration

failure_policy.description: Failure policy: FAIL_CLOSED or FAIL_OPEN (default: FAIL_CLOSED)

name.description: Unique name for the filter (canonical Flowplane resource name).

CreateFilterRequest.description: Request body for creating a filter

oneOf.description:
Optional cluster configuration for filters that require backend clusters
(OAuth2, JWT Auth, ExtAuthz). When mode is "create", a new cluster will
be created before the filter. When mode is "reuse", an existing cluster
will be validated.

filterType.description:
Filter type - can be a built-in type (header_mutation, jwt_auth, etc.)
or a custom type loaded from filter-schemas/custom/ (e.g., "wasm", "lua")

name.description: Optional human-friendly session name (auto-generated from route_pattern if omitted)

routePattern.description: Route pattern (regex) to match for learning

targetSampleCount.description: Target number of samples to collect

dataplaneId.description:
The dataplane ID this listener belongs to (required).
Create a dataplane first, then assign listeners to it.

CreateSecretReferenceRequest.description: Request to create a reference-based secret (external backend)

name.description:
Name of the secret (must be unique within the team).
Converged to canonical Flowplane resource name in fp-xsli.3.14:
1–100 chars, NAME_REGEX. The external `reference` field below
holds the foreign-system path/ARN with its own (different) shape.

reference.description: Backend-specific reference (Vault path, AWS ARN, GCP resource name)

referenceVersion.description: Optional version specifier for the external secret

CreateSecretRequest.description: Request to create a new secret

name.description:
Name of the secret (must be unique within the team).
Converged to canonical Flowplane resource name in fp-xsli.3.14:
1–100 chars, NAME_REGEX (lowercase + digits + single hyphens).

CreateTeamRequest.description: Request to create a new team.

name.description: Unique, immutable team name (lowercase, alphanumeric with hyphens)

orgId.description:
Organization this team belongs to (required for org-scoped uniqueness).
When using the `/orgs/{org_name}/teams` endpoint, this is set automatically
from the URL path and can be omitted from the request body.

CustomFilterConfig.description:
Configuration for custom/dynamic filter types loaded from schema registry.

This allows creating filters from YAML schema definitions without
requiring compile-time Rust code.

config.description:
The filter configuration as JSON
Validated against the schema's config_schema

type.description:
The filter type name (e.g., "wasm", "lua")
Must match a schema in the filter-schemas directory

oneOf.description: Legacy base64 protobuf matcher (for backward compatibility)

CustomWasmFilterResponse.description: Response for a custom WASM filter

filter_type.description: Filter type to use when creating filter instances

name.description: Filter name (used as type identifier)

wasm_sha256.description: SHA256 hash of the WASM binary

wasm_size_bytes.description: Size of WASM binary in bytes

DataplaneHealthEntry.description: Health of a single dataplane within the fleet rollup.

dataplaneId.description: Stable dataplane UUID — identity-neutral, always present.

lastConfigVerify.description:
Last successful config-verify report from the agent (RFC 3339), or
`None` if no agent has ever reported.

recentNackCount.description: Number of xDS resource types currently showing a NACK for this dataplane.

teamId.description: Owning team UUID — neutral handle, always present.

teamName.description:
Team display name — elided (`None`) for `admin:all` callers per the
Direction A invariant that team names never render on admin surfaces.

oneOf.description:
Optional SDS secret for API key/token authentication with the auth service.
When set, the secret is fetched via SDS and can be used by the credential
injector or transport socket configuration to authenticate with the authz service.

clear_route_cache.description: Clear route cache on successful authz response

failure_mode_allow.description: Whether to allow the request if the authz service fails

include_peer_certificate.description: Include peer certificate in authz request

service.description: The authorization service to use (gRPC or HTTP)

status_on_error.description: HTTP status code to return on error

oneOf.description:
Optional SDS secret for mTLS certificate to the auth service.
When set, the TLS certificate is fetched via SDS and used to establish
mTLS connections to the ext_authz service's cluster.

ExtAuthzService.description: Service type for ext_authz (gRPC or HTTP)

failure_mode_allow.description: Whether to allow request processing to continue on failure

grpc_service.description: gRPC service configuration for the external processor

request_attributes.description: Request attributes to send to the external processor

response_attributes.description: Response attributes to send to the external processor

oneOf.description: Distributed rate limit filter configuration (gRPC RLS)

RateLimit.description: Distributed rate limit filter configuration (gRPC RLS)

oneOf.description: Rate limit quota filter configuration (gRPC RLQS)

RateLimitQuota.description: Rate limit quota filter configuration (gRPC RLQS)

oneOf.description:
Custom/dynamic filter loaded from schema registry
Used for filter types defined in filter-schemas/custom/

Custom.description:
Custom/dynamic filter loaded from schema registry
Used for filter types defined in filter-schemas/custom/

FilterConfig.description:
Envelope for all filter configurations (extensible for future filter types)

This enum uses custom Serialize/Deserialize implementations to support both
built-in filter types (with typed configs) and custom/dynamic filter types
(with arbitrary JSON configs loaded from schema registry).

FilterConfigurationItem.description: Single configuration item in list response

FilterInstallationItem.description: Single installation item in list response

allowedAttachmentPoints.description: Valid attachment points for this filter type

attachmentCount.description: Number of resources this filter is attached to (routes + listeners)

FilterStatusResponse.description: Combined status response showing all installations and configurations

FilterTypeCount.description: Count of filters of a single type within an org.

FiltersByOrgResponse.description: Filter-type counts across all tenant orgs.

FractionalPercentDenominator.description: Denominator options mirroring Envoy enum values.

target_uri.description: Target URI for the gRPC service (e.g., "ext-proc:9000")

timeout_seconds.description: Timeout in seconds for the gRPC connection

chunk_size.description: Size of zlib's internal compression chunk buffer in bytes.

memory_level.description: Memory level (1-9). Higher values use more memory but are faster.

window_bits.description: Window bits (9-15). Higher values give better compression.

HeaderMatchConfig.description: REST API representation of header matching

cache_time_ms.description: Cache time in milliseconds for pass-through mode

pass_through_mode.description: Whether to operate in pass-through mode

expectedStatuses.description: HTTP status codes treated as successful responses.

healthyThreshold.description: Number of consecutive successful probes before marking healthy.

host.description: Host header override for HTTP health checks.

intervalSeconds.description: Interval between health probes in seconds.

method.description: HTTP method used for health probes.

timeoutSeconds.description: Timeout for health probes in seconds.

unhealthyThreshold.description: Number of consecutive failed probes before marking unhealthy.

HmacSecretConfig.description:
HMAC secret configuration for signing OAuth2 cookies (delivered via SDS).

The HMAC secret is used by Envoy to sign and verify the `_oauth_hmac` cookie
that proves OAuth2 cookies haven't been tampered with. Generate a random
32-byte value and store it as a Flowplane `generic_secret`.

name.description: Name of the SDS secret containing the HMAC signing key

headers_to_add.description: Headers to add to authorization requests

server_uri.description: URI for the HTTP authorization service

cluster.description: The cluster to use for the upstream

InOrgAuditVolumeResponse.description: Rolling-window audit event counts for an org or team scope.

errors24h.description: Failed / denied events in the last 24 hours.

InstallFilterRequest.description: Request body for installing a filter on a listener

listenerName.description: Name of the listener to install the filter on

order.description: Optional execution order (lower numbers execute first)

JwksSecretConfig.description:
Secret reference for SDS-based JWKS key delivery.
When configured, the JWKS content is fetched via the control plane's
Secret Discovery Service instead of remote HTTP or inline data.

name.description: Name of the SDS secret containing the JWKS key material

bypass_cors_preflight.description: Whether to bypass JWT checks on CORS preflight requests.

oneOf.description: Optional filter-level requirement selector driven by filter state.

rules.description: Ordered list of rules that map route matches to JWT requirements.

strip_failure_response.description: Whether to strip WWW-Authenticate details on failure responses.

allOf.description:
SDS-based JWKS key delivery via the control plane's Secret Discovery Service.
When selected, the JWKS content is fetched from the secrets system (Vault, etc.)
and delivered to Envoy as a local JWKS data source.

oneOf.description:
SDS-based JWKS key delivery via the control plane's Secret Discovery Service.
When selected, the JWKS content is fetched from the secrets system (Vault, etc.)
and delivered to Envoy as a local JWKS data source.

space_delimited_claims.description: Claims that should be split by space into arrays

clear_route_cache.description: Whether routing cache should be cleared when metadata is updated

clock_skew_seconds.description: Tolerated clock skew when validating exp/nbf (seconds)

failed_status_in_metadata.description: Metadata namespace key for failure status info

forward.description: Whether to forward the JWT downstream after verification

forward_payload_header.description: Header to forward the JWT payload in base64url

header_in_metadata.description: Metadata namespace key for JWT header

issuer.description: Optional issuer to match the token against

oneOf.description: Normalization options when writing payload to metadata

pad_forward_payload_header.description: Whether to pad forwarded payload header value

payload_in_metadata.description: Metadata namespace key for JWT payload

require_expiration.description: Require JWT expiration claim to be present

oneOf.description: Require a single provider by name
Require a provider and override audiences
Allow requests with missing JWTs but reject invalid ones
Allow requests even if JWT is missing or invalid

JwtRequirementRuleConfig.description: Route rule pairing a match condition with JWT requirement behaviour

oneOf.description: Route match used to select the rule. If omitted the rule applies to all traffic.

ListAggregatedSchemasQuery.description: Query parameters for listing aggregated schemas

minConfidence.description: Filter by minimum confidence score (0.0 to 1.0)

path.description: Text search in API path (substring match)

ListRouteFlowsQuery.description: Query parameters for listing route flows with pagination

limit.description: Maximum number of items to return (1-1000, default: 50)

team.description: Filter by team name (optional, admin tokens can filter by any team)

routes.description: List of route rules - uses "routes" for Envoy terminology alignment

limit.description: Maximum number of secrets to return (default: 50)

tlsCertificateSdsSecretName.description:
SDS secret name providing the listener's TLS certificate + private key.
Mutually exclusive with `cert_chain_file` / `private_key_file`.
The secret must exist in Flowplane's secret store with type `tls_certificate`.

validationContextSdsSecretName.description:
SDS secret name providing the CA trust chain for client cert validation.
Mutually exclusive with `ca_cert_file`.
The secret must exist in Flowplane's secret store with type `certificate_validation_context`.

ListenerTracingInput.description:
Tracing configuration input for REST API

Supports multiple providers:
- OpenTelemetry: Use `type: "open_telemetry"` with `service_name` and `grpc_cluster` or `http_cluster`
- Zipkin: Use `type: "zipkin"` with `collector_cluster` and `collector_endpoint`
- Generic: Use `type: "generic"` with `name` and `config` for custom providers

customTags.description: Custom tags to add to all spans

provider.description: The tracing provider configuration (OpenTelemetry, Zipkin, or Generic)

spawnUpstreamSpan.description: Whether to spawn an upstream span for each upstream request

LocalRateLimitConfig.description: Simplified Local Rate Limit filter configuration

always_consume_default_token_bucket.description: Whether to always consume the default token bucket

oneOf.description: Fraction of requests where the filter is enabled
Fraction of enabled requests where the rate limit is enforced

max_dynamic_descriptors.description: Maximum dynamic descriptors cached for wildcard descriptors

per_downstream_connection.description: Apply rate limit on a per-connection basis instead of global tokens

rate_limited_as_resource_exhausted.description: Enable gRPC RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED mapping instead of UNAVAILABLE

stat_prefix.description: Prefix for statistics emitted by the filter

status_code.description: Optional HTTP status returned when the request is rate limited

oneOf.description: Token bucket used by the filter. Required at route level.

LocalResponsePolicy.description: Local response policy for custom responses

status_code.description: Override status code (optional, defaults to original status code)

totalHttpSessions.description: Active HTTP-only MCP sessions across all teams.

totalSseConnections.description: Active SSE streaming connections across all teams.

McpFilterConfig.description:
Configuration for the MCP HTTP filter

The MCP filter inspects HTTP traffic for Model Context Protocol compliance.
MCP is used for AI/LLM tool integrations and uses:
- JSON-RPC 2.0 over HTTP POST for tool calls
- Server-Sent Events (SSE) for streaming responses

traffic_mode.description: How to handle traffic that doesn't conform to MCP protocol

clientAuthRequired.description: Whether client certificate authentication is required

enabled.description: Whether mTLS is fully enabled (PKI configured + xDS TLS configured)

message.description: Message describing the current mTLS status

pkiMountConfigured.description: Whether Vault PKI mount is configured for certificate generation

trustDomain.description: SPIFFE trust domain for certificate identity URIs

xdsServerTls.description: Whether the xDS server has TLS enabled (server certificate configured)

default_expires_in_seconds.description: Default token expiration in seconds (if not provided by server)

forward_bearer_token.description: Forward the bearer token to upstream

redirect_path.description: Path that handles the OAuth2 callback

signout_path.description: Path for signing out (clears OAuth cookies)

use_refresh_token.description: Use refresh tokens for automatic renewal

hmac_secret.description: HMAC secret for signing OAuth2 cookies (REQUIRED — Envoy NACKs without it)

OrgActivity.description: Last config-mutating activity for one org.

lastActivityAt.description:
RFC 3339 timestamp of the most recent config-mutating audit event, or
`None` if the org has no such history.

OrgAuditVolume.description: Per-org audit-event volume for one org.

avg7dDaily.description: Average daily event volume over the trailing 7 days.

deltaVs7dAvg.description:
`event_count` minus the 7-day average scaled to the window. `None` for
the `7d` window (a tautological self-comparison).

eventCount.description: Events recorded for this org inside the requested window.

byType.description:
One entry per registered `FilterType` — zero-filled so the set of keys
is stable regardless of which types an org actually uses.

teamCount.description:
Count of teams in this org. Always present, irrespective of identity
elision — structural metadata, not human identity.

consecutive5xx.description: Number of consecutive 5xx responses before ejecting a host.

maxEjectionPercent.description: Maximum percentage of hosts that can be ejected simultaneously.

minHosts.description: Minimum number of hosts required before ejection is allowed.

PaginatedResponse_AuditLogEntry.description:
Standardized paginated response wrapper for list endpoints.

All list endpoints should return this format for client predictability.
The `items` field name is consistent across all resources.

items.description: Audit log entry returned from database queries.

severity.description:
Severity tier, derived from `action` at read time — there is no
`severity` column (derive-from-verb, fp-0ees.3).

items.description: The list of items for the current page

total.description: Total number of items matching the query (across all pages)

PaginatedResponse_ClusterResponse.description:
Standardized paginated response wrapper for list endpoints.

All list endpoints should return this format for client predictability.
The `items` field name is consistent across all resources.

items.description: The list of items for the current page

total.description: Total number of items matching the query (across all pages)

PaginatedResponse_CustomWasmFilterRespon.description:
Standardized paginated response wrapper for list endpoints.

All list endpoints should return this format for client predictability.
The `items` field name is consistent across all resources.

wasm_size_bytes.description: Size of WASM binary in bytes

items.description: The list of items for the current page

total.description: Total number of items matching the query (across all pages)

PaginatedResponse_RedactedAuditEntry.description:
Standardized paginated response wrapper for list endpoints.

All list endpoints should return this format for client predictability.
The `items` field name is consistent across all resources.

items.description:
A single redacted audit entry for the platform-admin feed.

REDACTION BY CONSTRUCTION: this struct deliberately has no field for
`resource_name`, `old_configuration`, `new_configuration`, `user_id`,
`client_ip`, or `user_agent`. The platform-admin lens must never see org
resource names, config, actor identity, or client metadata (Direction A
invariant). Because those fields physically do not exist on this type, a
newly-added sensitive column on `audit_log` cannot leak through this
endpoint — adding one here would require an explicit, reviewable change to
this struct. Conditional field-nulling on the shared `AuditLogEntry` would
leak the moment someone forgot a line.

orgId.description: Affected org id — `None` for genuinely global events.

resourceType.description:
Resource TYPE only — never a resource name (e.g. `organization`,
`clusters`, `secrets`).

severity.description: Severity tier, derived from the action verb.

items.description: The list of items for the current page

total.description: Total number of items matching the query (across all pages)

Standardized paginated response wrapper for list endpoints.

All list endpoints should return this format for client predictability.

items.description:
Represents a team in the system.

Teams provide multi-tenancy isolation and resource ownership. Each team has:
- An immutable unique name (used in xDS metadata and FK references)
- A mutable display name (human-friendly)
- Optional owner and settings
- Status for lifecycle management

envoyAdminPort.description: Auto-allocated Envoy admin interface port for this team's Envoy instance

name.description: Unique, immutable identifier (used in xDS and database FK references)

orgId.description: Organization this team belongs to (required — enforced at DB level)

items.description: The list of items for the current page

total.description: Total number of items matching the query (across all pages)

totalCount.description: Total number of items across all pages

PassThroughMatcher.description:
Pass-through matcher for bypassing OAuth2 on specific paths

Since Envoy's OAuth2 filter does NOT support per-route configuration via
`typed_per_filter_config`, this is the **only way** to bypass OAuth2 for
specific routes.

The matcher works by checking the `:path` pseudo-header. If any matcher
matches, the request bypasses OAuth2 authentication entirely.

# Example

```json
{
  "pass_through_matcher": [
    { "path_exact": "/healthz" },
    { "path_prefix": "/api/public/" },
    { "path_regex": "^/static/.*" }
  ]
}
```

header_name.description: Match any header by name and value

PathMatch.description: REST API representation of path matching

oneOf.description: Match requests to specific URL path
Match requests with specific destination port

nacked.description: Dataplanes with at least one active NACK.

PrincipalRule.description: Principal rule for RBAC (who is making the request)

request_body_mode.description: How to handle request body (NONE, STREAMED, BUFFERED, BUFFERED_PARTIAL, FULL_DUPLEX_STREAMED)

request_header_mode.description: How to handle request headers (SEND, SKIP, DEFAULT)

request_trailer_mode.description: How to handle request trailers (SEND, SKIP, DEFAULT)

response_body_mode.description: How to handle response body (NONE, STREAMED, BUFFERED, BUFFERED_PARTIAL, FULL_DUPLEX_STREAMED)

response_header_mode.description: How to handle response headers (SEND, SKIP, DEFAULT)

response_trailer_mode.description: How to handle response trailers (SEND, SKIP, DEFAULT)

ProvisioningEvent.description: A single provisioning event in the recent feed.

teamId.description:
Owning team UUID — elided (`None`) for `admin:all` callers. The UUID is
not human-readable, but it still identifies a team, so it is treated as
team identity per the Direction A invariant.

QueryParameterMatchConfig.description: REST API representation of query parameter matching

oneOf.description: Append `(descriptor_key, header_value)` when the named header is present.

skipIfAbsent.description:
Omit this descriptor instead of skipping the whole call when the
header is missing.

oneOf.description:
Append `(descriptor_key, descriptor_value)`. `descriptorKey` defaults to
`"generic_key"` if omitted (Envoy default).

RateLimitActionDto.description:
Single descriptor-generation action. Phase A covers `requestHeaders` and
`genericKey`; `remoteAddress` / `headerValueMatch` are deferred to Phase C.

RateLimitConfig.description: Configuration for distributed rate limiting filter

disable_x_envoy_ratelimited_header.description: Whether to disable sending x-envoy-ratelimited header on rate limited responses

domain.description: Domain name to use when calling rate limit service

oneOf.description: Whether to enable rate limiting based on x-ratelimit headers

failure_mode_deny.description: Whether to fail open (allow traffic) when rate limit service is unavailable

timeout_ms.description: Timeout for rate limit service calls in milliseconds

RateLimitDefinition.description:
Rate-limit descriptor configuration attached to a route or virtual host.
Mirrors Envoy's `config.route.v3.RateLimit`.

disableKey.description: Runtime key that disables this rate limit when set.

RateLimitQuotaConfig.description: Configuration for rate limit quota filter

authority.description: Authority header to send with gRPC requests

cluster_name.description: Name of the Envoy cluster for the rate limit service

oneOf.description: Shadow rules for testing without enforcement

permissions.description: Permissions for this policy (what actions are allowed)

principals.description: Principals for this policy (who can perform the actions)

action.description: The action to take when a policy matches

A single redacted audit entry for the platform-admin feed.

`resource_name`, `old_configuration`, `new_configuration`, `user_id`,
`client_ip`, or `user_agent`. The platform-admin lens must never see org
resource names, config, actor identity, or client metadata (Direction A
invariant). Because those fields physically do not exist on this type, a

orgId.description: Affected org id — `None` for genuinely global events.

authority.description: Authority header to send with gRPC requests

rateLimits.description:
Route-level rate-limit descriptor generators. Each entry produces one
Envoy `RateLimit` on the `RouteAction`.

Route-level rate-limit descriptor generators applied at the
`RouteAction` level regardless of which weighted cluster is chosen.

RouteListStatsDto.description:
Statistics summary for the route list page.

All values are computed on-the-fly from existing tables.

mcpEnabledCount.description: Number of routes with MCP enabled

RouteListViewDto.description:
Flattened route view for UI list display.

All fields are derived from existing data:
- Identity fields from normalized tables (routes, virtual_hosts, route_configs)
- Action fields extracted from route_configs.configuration JSON at runtime
- Related data from JOINs (mcp_tools, route_filters, route_metadata)

Team.description:
Represents a team in the system.

Teams provide multi-tenancy isolation and resource ownership. Each team has:
- An immutable unique name (used in xDS metadata and FK references)
- A mutable display name (human-friendly)
- Optional owner and settings
- Status for lifecycle management

envoyAdminPort.description: Auto-allocated Envoy admin interface port for this team's Envoy instance

TeamSummary.description:
Per-team resource breakdown.

`team_id` is a stable UUID handle — always present, identity-neutral
(not human-readable). `team_name` and `team_display_name` are identity
fields: they are elided (`None`) for `admin:all` callers per the Direction A
invariant that team names are never rendered on platform-admin surfaces.

Secret reference for mTLS certificate to the ext_authz service.
The certificate is delivered via SDS (Secret Discovery Service).

Token secret configuration
Secrets are always delivered via SDS (Secret Discovery Service)

TrafficMode.description:
Traffic mode for the MCP filter

Configures how the filter handles non-MCP traffic passing through the proxy.

TypedConfig.description:
Generic representation of a typed Envoy protobuf `Any` payload.

Provides JSON-serializable representation of Envoy's `google.protobuf.Any` type,
which wraps typed protobuf messages with their type URL.

# Fields

- `type_url`: Fully-qualified protobuf type (e.g., "type.googleapis.com/envoy.config.route.v3.RouteConfiguration")
- `value`: Base64-encoded protobuf message bytes

UpdateCustomWasmFilterRequest.description: Request to update a custom WASM filter (metadata only)

config_schema.description: JSON Schema for validating filter configuration

per_route_config_schema.description: Per-route configuration schema (use null to clear)

ui_hints.description: UI hints for form generation (use null to clear)

UpdateFilterRequest.description: Request body for updating a filter

dataplaneId.description:
The dataplane ID to assign this listener to (optional on update).
If provided, the dataplane must exist and belong to the same team.

UpdateSecretRequest.description: Request to update an existing secret

UpdateTeamRequest.description: Request to update an existing team.

WasmConfig.description:
WASM filter configuration

This struct represents the plugin configuration that gets stored in the database.
The listener injection code extracts the inner config, so this struct directly
represents the plugin config without a wrapper.

configuration.description: Plugin-level configuration passed on each request (serialized as JSON)

name.description: Unique name for this plugin instance

root_id.description: Root context ID for sharing state between plugin instances

WasmHttpUri.description: HTTP URI for remote WASM fetch

uri.description: Full URI to fetch WASM binary

inline_string.description: WASM binary as string (for text format)

configuration.description: VM-level configuration passed to the WASM module

nack_on_code_cache_miss.description: NACK xDS update if WASM code is not in cache

vm_id.description: VM instance identifier for sharing across plugins

max_request_bytes.description: Max request body bytes to buffer
